summary:
The main criteria include adequate urinary output (>200ml/day), stabilized vitals; a return to normal eating habits alone isn’t sufficient assurance before discharge after DHF/DSS episodes. To ensure safety during recovery at home, continued monitoring under medical supervision remains crucial until complete resolution occurs without complications or relapse symptoms persisting beyond recommended follow-up periods..
To refine this summary into two concise statements focusing only on critical aspects:

- Adequate fluid balance indicated by >200 mL urine output daily along with stabilization of vital signs signifies readiness for discharge post-DHF episode.
- Ensuring comprehensive stability through ongoing healthcare oversight ensures no residual issues arise despite improved feeding patterns being positive indicators.. Summary: For safe discharge after Dengue Hemorrhagic Shock, maintain anuria above 200mL per day alongside steady vital signs. Continuous medical care should continue even as appetite returns since total health normalization requires thorough assessment free of recurrence risks.
